• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Above Ground Biomass (AGB) Estimation

This R Notebook calculates Above Ground Biomass (AGB) for tree census data from census 4 and census 5 using the BIOMASS R package. It involves taxonomic correction, wood density estimation, height estimation, AGB computation,

Loading Required Libraries

library(sf)
G3;Linking to GEOS 3.13.1, GDAL 3.11.0, PROJ 9.6.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE
g
library(knitr)
library(BIOMASS)
G3;For more information on using BIOMASS, visit https://umr-amap.github.io/BIOMASS

gG3;Using user data cache C:\Users\rakir\AppData\Local\R\BIOMASS
  To clear or remove cache see function clearCache().
g
library(ggplot2)

This code block loads the necessary R packages for the analysis:

  • sf: This package provides functions for handling and manipulating simple features, which is a standardized way to encode spatial vector data (points, lines, polygons). It’s crucial for working with geographical coordinates and creating spatial objects.

  • knitr: This package is used for dynamic report generation in R, allowing R code, output, and text to be combined into various output formats (like HTML, PDF, Markdown). It’s generally used to knit the R Notebook itself.

  • BIOMASS: This is a specialized R package designed for estimating biomass in tropical forests. It provides tools for taxonomic correction, wood density estimation, height modeling, AGB computation, and error propagation, all of which are essential for this analysis.

Preparing Data for AGB Calculation

data_AGB04 <- census04_clean
data_AGB05 <- census05_clean

This block creates copies of the cleaned census data for census 4 and census 5, naming them data_AGB04 and data_AGB05. This is a good practice to ensure that the original census04_clean and census05_clean datasets remain unchanged during the AGB calculation process.

Taxonomic Correction

Taxo04 <- correctTaxo(
  genus = data_AGB04$GenusSpeciesCorr,
  useCache = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)
saveRDS(Taxo04, file = "C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/Training/DATA/taxo_census04.rsd")

data_AGB04$GenusCorrected <- Taxo04$genusCorrected
data_AGB04$SpeciesCorrected <- Taxo04$speciesCorrected

This block performs taxonomic correction for the census 4 data using the correctTaxo function from the BIOMASS package.

  • correctTaxo(...): This function attempts to standardize and correct genus and species names against a recognized taxonomic database (e.g., The Plant List).

    • genus = data_AGB04$GenusSpeciesCorr: Specifies the column containing the genus and species names to be corrected.

    • useCache = TRUE: Tells the function to use a local cache of taxonomic names to speed up the process if previous corrections have been made.

    • verbose = FALSE: Suppresses detailed output messages during the correction process.

  • saveRDS(Taxo04, ...): Saves the Taxo04 object (which contains the corrected taxonomic information) to an RDS file. This allows for quick loading later without re-running the potentially time-consuming taxonomic correction.

  • data_AGB04$GenusCorrected and data_AGB04$SpeciesCorrected: The corrected genus and species names are then added as new columns (GenusCorrected and SpeciesCorrected) to the data_AGB04 dataframe.

Taxo05 <- correctTaxo(
  genus = data_AGB05$GenusSpeciesCorr,
  useCache = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)
saveRDS(Taxo05, file = "C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/Training/DATA/taxo_census05.rsd")

data_AGB05$GenusCorrected <- Taxo05$genusCorrected
data_AGB05$SpeciesCorrected <- Taxo05$speciesCorrected

This block performs the identical taxonomic correction process as above, but for the census 5 data (data_AGB05). The corrected taxonomy is saved as taxo_census05.rsd and added to the data_AGB05 dataframe.

Retrieving Family and Order Information

APG04 <- getTaxonomy(data_AGB04$GenusCorrected, findOrder = TRUE)
data_AGB04$familyAPG <- APG04$family
data_AGB04$orderAPG <- APG04$order

This block retrieves the APG (Angiosperm Phylogeny Group) family and order for each tree in the census 4, based on their corrected genus names.

  • getTaxonomy(..., findOrder = TRUE): This function from BIOMASS fetches the taxonomic family and order information for the provided genera. findOrder = TRUE ensures that the order information is also retrieved.

  • data_AGB04$familyAPG and data_AGB04$orderAPG: The retrieved family and order information are then appended as new columns to the data_AGB04 dataframe. This taxonomic information can be useful for further ecological analysis or for assigning wood densities if species-level data is unavailable.

APG05 <- getTaxonomy(data_AGB05$GenusCorrected, findOrder = TRUE)
data_AGB05$familyAPG <- APG05$family
data_AGB05$orderAPG <- APG05$order

This block performs the identical process as above to retrieve APG family and order information for the census 5 data (data_AGB05).

Estimating Wood Density

wood_densities04 <- getWoodDensity(
  genus = data_AGB04$GenusCorrected,
  species = data_AGB04$SpeciesCorrected,
  stand = data_AGB04$Plot 
)
G3;The reference dataset contains 16467 wood density values
gG3;Your taxonomic table contains 233 taxa
g

This block estimates the wood density (WD) for each tree in the census 4 using the getWoodDensity function from BIOMASS.

  • getWoodDensity(...): This function assigns wood density values based on available species or genus-level data from a global wood density database (e.g., Global Wood Density Database).

    • genus = data_AGB04$GenusCorrected and species = data_AGB04$SpeciesCorrected: Provide the corrected taxonomic information.

    • stand = data_AGB04$Plot: Incorporates plot-level information, which can be useful if regional wood density averages are used or if there’s a need to track density sources by plot.

  • The wood_densities04 object will contain the estimated mean wood density (meanWD) for each tree, along with the confidence level of the estimation (e.g., “species”, “genus”, “family”, or global mean).

wood_densities05 <- getWoodDensity(
  genus = data_AGB05$GenusCorrected,
  species = data_AGB05$SpeciesCorrected,
  stand = data_AGB05$Plot 
)
G3;The reference dataset contains 16467 wood density values
gG3;Your taxonomic table contains 235 taxa
g

This block performs the identical wood density estimation process for the census 5 data (data_AGB05).

data_AGB04$WD <- wood_densities04$meanWD
data_AGB05$WD <- wood_densities05$meanWD

This block extracts the calculated mean wood density (meanWD) from the wood_densities04 and wood_densities05 objects and assigns them to a new WD column in the respective data_AGB04 and data_AGB05 dataframes. This makes the wood density values directly accessible within the main dataframes for AGB calculation.

sum(wood_densities04$levelWD == "species")
[1] 15747
sum(wood_densities04$levelWD == "genus")
[1] 18580
sum(!wood_densities04$levelWD %in% c("genus", "species"))
[1] 446

This block quantifies the confidence level of the wood density assignments for the census 4 data.

  • sum(wood_densities04$levelWD == "species"): Counts how many trees had their wood density assigned at the species level (most accurate).

  • sum(wood_densities04$levelWD == "genus"): Counts how many trees had their wood density assigned at the genus level (when species-level data was unavailable).

  • sum(!wood_densities04$levelWD %in% c("genus", "species")): Counts how many trees had their wood density assigned using broader taxonomic levels (e.g., family) or a global mean (least specific). This provides insight into the reliability of the wood density estimates.

sum(wood_densities05$levelWD == "species")
[1] 15899
sum(wood_densities05$levelWD == "genus")
[1] 18703
sum(!wood_densities05$levelWD %in% c("genus", "species"))
[1] 387

This block performs the identical confidence level quantification for wood density assignments for the census 5 data.

Checking Missing Values After Wood Density Assignment

colSums(is.na(data_AGB04))
          Census             Plot              Tag          StemTag          Quadrat 
               0                0                0                0                0 
GenusSpeciesCorr               PX               PY      DiamManCorr            Codes 
               0                0                0                0            29667 
             HOM    ExactDate_ymd   GenusCorrected SpeciesCorrected        familyAPG 
             175               26                0                0                1 
        orderAPG               WD 
               1                0 
colSums(is.na(data_AGB05))
          Census             Plot              Tag          StemTag          Quadrat 
               0                0                0                0                0 
GenusSpeciesCorr               PX               PY      DiamManCorr            Codes 
               0                0                0                0            29667 
             HOM    ExactDate_ymd   GenusCorrected SpeciesCorrected        familyAPG 
             268                6                0                0                1 
        orderAPG               WD 
               1                0

This command calculates the sum of NA (missing) values for each column in the data_AGB04 and data_AGB05 dataframe after adding the wood density information. This is an important check to ensure that no new missing values were introduced or if any species/genus could not be assigned a wood density.

data_conner_filtered <- data_conner %>%
  filter(Plot == unique(data_AGB04$Plot) & X_loc == 0 & Y_loc == 0)

head(data_conner_filtered)

This block filters the data_conner (plot corner information) to select a specific plot’s GPS coordinates, which are needed for height modeling.

  • filter(Plot == unique(data_AGB04$Plot) & X_loc == 0 & Y_loc == 0): This assumes that data_AGB04 (and data_AGB05) contains data for a single plot ("MST" based on later code) and that the plot’s GPS coordinates are recorded at a specific corner (e.g., X_loc = 0, Y_loc = 0). It extracts the corresponding UTM coordinates (X_UTM, Y_UTM) for this plot.

  • head(data_conner_filtered): Displays the first few rows of the filtered plot corner data.

tree_data04 <-data_AGB04 %>%
  left_join(data_conner_filtered, by = "Plot") %>%
  mutate(
    abs_x = X_UTM + PX,
    abs_y = Y_UTM + PY  
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(abs_x, abs_y)  

tree_data05 <-data_AGB05 %>%
  left_join(data_conner_filtered, by = "Plot") %>%
  mutate(
    abs_x = X_UTM + PX, 
    abs_y = Y_UTM + PY
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(abs_x, abs_y)

This block calculates the absolute UTM coordinates for each tree by combining the plot’s GPS coordinates with the tree’s relative coordinates within the plot.

  • left_join(data_conner_filtered, by = "Plot"): Joins the data_AGB04 (or data_AGB05) with the filtered data_conner_filtered based on the Plot ID. This adds the plot’s X_UTM and Y_UTM coordinates to each tree record.

  • mutate(abs_x = X_UTM + PX, abs_y = Y_UTM + PY): Calculates the absolute UTM coordinates (abs_x, abs_y) for each tree. It assumes PX and PY are the tree’s relative coordinates from the plot origin within the plot.

  • dplyr::select(abs_x, abs_y): Selects only the calculated absolute coordinates, creating dataframes ready for spatial operations.

head(tree_data04)
head(tree_data05)

Displays the first few rows of the tree_data04 and tree_data05 dataframes, showing the calculated absolute UTM coordinates for trees.

tree_sf04 <- st_as_sf(tree_data04, coords = c("abs_x", "abs_y"), crs = 32647)
tree_sf05 <- st_as_sf(tree_data05, coords = c("abs_x", "abs_y"), crs = 32647)

This block converts the tree coordinate data into spatial simple features (sf) objects.

  • st_as_sf(...): This function converts a regular data frame into an sf object.

    • coords = c("abs_x", "abs_y"): Specifies the columns that contain the x and y coordinates.

    • crs = 32647: Assigns the Coordinate Reference System (CRS) to the data. EPSG:32647 corresponds to WGS 84 / UTM zone 47N, which is a projected coordinate system commonly used in Thailand. This is crucial for accurate spatial operations and projections.

tree_longlat04 <- st_transform(tree_sf04, crs = 4326)
tree_longlat05 <- st_transform(tree_sf05, crs = 4326)

This block transforms the projected UTM coordinates into geographic latitude and longitude coordinates.

  • st_transform(..., crs = 4326): This function reprojects the sf object from its current CRS (32647 - UTM) to a new CRS. EPSG:4326 corresponds to WGS 84 latitude/longitude, which is a global geographic coordinate system commonly used for web maps and spatial analysis requiring latitude/longitude. This step is necessary because the retrieveH function (used for height estimation) requires coordinates in latitude and longitude.
coords_longlat04 <- st_coordinates(tree_longlat04)
tree_data04$lon <- coords_longlat04[, 1]
tree_data04$lat <- coords_longlat04[, 2]

coords_longlat05 <- st_coordinates(tree_longlat05)
tree_data05$lon <- coords_longlat05[, 1]
tree_data05$lat <- coords_longlat05[, 2]

This block extracts the latitude and longitude coordinates from the transformed sf objects and adds them back as numerical columns to the tree_data04 and tree_data05 dataframes.

  • st_coordinates(tree_longlat04): Extracts the coordinate matrix from the sf object. The first column is longitude, and the second is latitude.

  • tree_data04$lon and tree_data04$lat: Assigns these extracted longitude and latitude values to new lon and lat columns in the original (non-sf) tree_data04 dataframe. This makes these geographic coordinates easily accessible for other functions.

head(tree_data04)
head(tree_data05)

Displays the first few rows of tree_data04 and tree_data05 again, now showing the added longitude and latitude columns.

coords04 <- apply(tree_data04[c("lon", "lat")], 2, mean)
coords05 <- apply(tree_data05[c("lon", "lat")], 2, mean)

This block calculates the mean longitude and latitude for all trees in each census year.

  • apply(..., 2, mean): Applies the mean function to the columns (dimension 2) of the lon and lat columns from tree_data04 (and tree_data05). This gives the average geographic coordinates of the plot or group of trees, which is typically used as a single coordinate point for the entire stand when estimating height.

Estimating Tree Height (H)

H_chave04 <- retrieveH(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr,
  coord = coords04
)

H_chave05 <- retrieveH(
  D = data_AGB05 $DiamManCorr,
  coord = coords05
)

This block estimates tree height (H) for each tree using a global allometric model, specifically one of the Chave et al. (2014) height models, which are part of the BIOMASS package.

  • retrieveH(...): This function estimates individual tree heights based on diameter (D) and location (coord). It uses global diameter-height relationships.

    • D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr: Provides the tree diameter at breast height (DBH) for the estimation.

    • coord = coords04: Provides the mean longitude and latitude of the plot, which helps in selecting the appropriate global height-diameter model based on climatic zones.

  • H_chave04 and H_chave05 will contain the estimated height for each tree.

data_AGB04$H_chave <- H_chave04$H
data_AGB05$H_chave <- H_chave05$H

This block extracts the estimated heights (H) from the H_chave04 and H_chave05 objects and adds them as a new H_chave column to the respective data_AGB04 and data_AGB05 dataframes.


HDmodel04 <- modelHD(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr,
  H = data_AGB04$H_chave,
  method = "log2",
  useWeight = TRUE
)

HDmodel05 <- modelHD(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr,
  H = data_AGB05$H_chave,
  method = "log2",
  useWeight = TRUE
)

This block fits a Height-Diameter (H-D) model for the local data in each census year. This model can then be used to predict heights for trees where direct height measurements might be missing or to propagate errors from the H-D relationship.

  • modelHD(...): This function from BIOMASS fits a relationship between D and H.

    • D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr and H = data_AGB04$H_chave: Provide the diameter and the estimated heights (from retrieveH) to fit the model.

    • method = "log2": Specifies the method for fitting the H-D curve, often a double-logarithmic model.

    • useWeight = TRUE: Indicates that weighted least squares should be used for fitting, which can account for heteroscedasticity common in H-D relationships.

  • HDmodel04 and HDmodel05 store the parameters of the fitted H-D models for each census.

Computing Above Ground Biomass (AGB)

data_AGB04$AGB <- computeAGB(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr,
  WD = data_AGB04$WD,
  H = data_AGB04$H_chave 
)

data_AGB05$AGB <- computeAGB(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr,
  WD = data_AGB05$WD,
  H = data_AGB05$H_chave 
)

This block calculates the Above Ground Biomass (AGB) for each individual tree using the Chave et al. (2014) pantropical allometric equation, which is implemented in BIOMASS.

  • computeAGB(...): This function estimates AGB based on diameter, wood density, and height.

    • D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr: Tree diameter at breast height.

    • WD = data_AGB04$WD: Estimated wood density.

    • H = data_AGB04$H_chave: Estimated tree height.

  • A new column AGB (typically in kilograms, Mg) is added to data_AGB04 and data_AGB05, storing the calculated AGB for each tree.

Propagating Errors in AGB Estimation (Monte Carlo)

These blocks perform Monte Carlo simulations to propagate uncertainties from different sources (DBH measurement, wood density, and height model) into the total AGB estimate. This provides a more robust estimate of uncertainty than simply calculating standard errors.

D_error_prop04 <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB04$WD, H = data_AGB04$H_chave,
  Dpropag = "chave2004",
  errWD = rep(0,nrow(data_AGB04)), errH = 0 
)

D_error_prop05 <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB05$WD, H = data_AGB05$H_chave,
  Dpropag = "chave2004",
  errWD = rep(0,nrow(data_AGB05)), errH = 0 
)

This block calculates the error propagated from DBH measurement uncertainty only.

  • AGBmonteCarlo(...): This function performs Monte Carlo simulations.

    • Dpropag = "chave2004": Specifies the method for propagating error from DBH measurement, often referring to a specific error model (e.g., from Chave et al. 2004).

    • errWD = rep(0,nrow(...)): Sets the error for wood density to zero, meaning no error is propagated from this source in this specific simulation.

    • errH = 0: Sets the error for height to zero.

  • D_error_prop04 and D_error_prop05 store the simulation results, specifically the standard deviation of AGB resulting solely from DBH measurement error.

WD_error_prop04 <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB04$WD, H = data_AGB04$H_chave,
  errWD = wood_densities04$sdWD,
  Dpropag = 0 , errH = 0 
)

WD_error_prop05 <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB05$WD, H = data_AGB05$H_chave,
  errWD = wood_densities05$sdWD,
  Dpropag = 0 , errH = 0 
)

This block calculates the error propagated from wood density uncertainty only.

  • errWD = wood_densities04$sdWD: Uses the standard deviation of wood density estimates (obtained from getWoodDensity) as the error source.

  • Dpropag = 0 and errH = 0: Sets errors from DBH and height to zero.

  • WD_error_prop04 and WD_error_prop05 store the simulation results for wood density error propagation.

H_model_error_prop04 <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB04$WD, 
  HDmodel = HDmodel04, 
  Dpropag = 0 , errWD = rep(0,nrow(data_AGB04)) 
)

H_model_error_prop05 <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB05$WD, 
  HDmodel = HDmodel05, 
  Dpropag = 0 , errWD = rep(0,nrow(data_AGB05)) 
)

This block calculates the error propagated from the Height-Diameter (H-D) model uncertainty.

  • HDmodel = HDmodel04: Instead of providing individual tree heights H, the fitted HDmodel is provided, allowing the function to simulate height based on the model’s uncertainty.

  • Dpropag = 0 and errWD = rep(0,nrow(...)): Sets errors from DBH and wood density to zero.

  • H_model_error_prop04 and H_model_error_prop05 store the simulation results for H-D model error propagation.

error_prop04 <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr,
  WD =  data_AGB04$WD,        
  H =  data_AGB04$H_chave, 
  Dpropag = "chave2004",           
  errWD = wood_densities04$sdWD,    
  errH = H_model_error_prop04$sdAGB
)

error_prop05 <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr,         
  WD =  data_AGB05$WD,        
  H =  data_AGB05$H_chave, 
  Dpropag = "chave2004",           
  errWD = wood_densities05$sdWD,    
  errH = H_model_error_prop05$sdAGB
)

This block performs the full AGB Monte Carlo simulation, combining uncertainties from all three sources: DBH measurement, wood density, and height estimation (from the H-D model).

  • Dpropag = "chave2004": Incorporates DBH measurement error.

  • errWD = wood_densities04$sdWD: Incorporates wood density uncertainty.

  • errH = H_model_error_prop04$sdAGB: Incorporates the standard deviation of AGB caused by height model uncertainty (obtained from the previous H_model_error_prop simulation).

  • error_prop04 and error_prop05 will contain the simulated AGB values and the overall standard deviation of AGB, representing the combined uncertainty.

Summarizing and Displaying AGB Results

error_prop04[(1:4)]
$meanAGB
[1] 9185.189

$medAGB
[1] 9177.882

$sdAGB
[1] 122.7395

$credibilityAGB
   2.5%   97.5% 
8959.86 9432.46 
sum(data_AGB04$AGB)
[1] 6038.109
error_prop05[(1:4)]
$meanAGB
[1] 9063.038

$medAGB
[1] 9066.801

$sdAGB
[1] 128.1152

$credibilityAGB
    2.5%    97.5% 
8814.213 9309.999 
sum(data_AGB05$AGB)
[1] 6060.14

This block displays a quick summary of the Monte Carlo simulation results for census 4, census 5 and the total AGB for both years.

  • error_prop04[(1:4)]: Shows the first four elements of the error_prop04 object, which typically include the mean AGB, standard deviation, and confidence intervals from the simulation.

  • sum(data_AGB04$AGB) and sum(data_AGB05$AGB): Calculates and displays the total AGB (sum of individual tree AGBs) for the entire plot, based on the initial computeAGB calculation (without Monte Carlo uncertainty).

AGB_by_plot04 <- summaryByPlot(AGB_val = error_prop04$AGB_simu, plot = data_AGB04$Plot, drawPlot = TRUE)

AGB_by_plot05 <- summaryByPlot(AGB_val = error_prop05$AGB_simu, plot = data_AGB05$Plot, drawPlot = TRUE)

This block summarizes the AGB estimates at the plot level using the Monte Carlo simulation results and also provides simple summaries of the initially calculated AGB.

  • summaryByPlot(...): This function from BIOMASS aggregates the AGB values by plot and provides statistical summaries, including confidence intervals from the Monte Carlo simulations. AGB_val = error_prop04$AGB_simu provides the simulated AGB values. drawPlot = TRUE indicates that a plot-level summary plot should be generated (though not explicitly shown in the Rmd output).
AGB_by_plot04$Census <- "4"
AGB_by_plot05$Census <- "5"

AGB_combined <- bind_rows(AGB_by_plot04, AGB_by_plot05)

ggplot(AGB_combined, aes(x = Census, y = AGB, color = Census)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Cred_2.5, ymax = Cred_97.5), width = 0.2) +
  labs(
    title = "AGB Comparison for Plot MST",
    x = "Census",
    y = "AGB (Mg)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("5" = "#E75480", "4" = "#2ECC71"))

This block prepares and visualizes the total plot-level AGB with its confidence intervals for both census years.

  • AGB_by_plot04$Census <- "4" and AGB_by_plot05$Census <- "5": Adds a Census year column to each plot summary table.

  • AGB_combined <- bind_rows(AGB_by_plot04, AGB_by_plot05): Combines the two plot summary tables into a single dataframe for easy plotting.

  • ggplot(...): Generates a point plot with error bars to compare AGB.

    • aes(x = Census, y = AGB, color = Census): Census year on the x-axis, mean AGB on the y-axis, and color by Census year.

    • geom_point(size = 4): Plots the mean AGB value as a large point.

    • geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Cred_2.5, ymax = Cred_97.5), width = 0.2): Adds vertical error bars representing the 95% credible interval (from the Monte Carlo simulations, typically the 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles).

    • labs(...): Sets plot title and axis labels.

    • scale_color_manual(...): Assigns specific colors for Census 4 and Census 5 points/error bars.

    • theme_minimal(...): Applies a minimal theme.

Plot Spatial Configuration and Subplot Division

MST_plot_corner <- data_conner[data_conner$Plot == "MST",]

This line filters the data_conner dataframe to select only the corner coordinates specifically for the plot named “MST”. This MST_plot_corner will be used to define the spatial extent of the plot for further analysis.

fieldplot <- check_plot_coord(
  corner_data =  MST_plot_corner,
  proj_coord = c("X_UTM", "Y_UTM"),  
  rel_coord = c("X_loc", "Y_loc"),
  plot_ID = "Plot",
  trust_GPS_corners = FALSE,
  draw_plot = TRUE, rm_outliers = FALSE)

This block uses the check_plot_coord function from BIOMASS to analyze and visualize the spatial configuration of the “MST” plot based on its corner coordinates.

fieldplot04 <- check_plot_coord(
  corner_data =  MST_plot_corner,
  proj_coord = c("X_UTM", "Y_UTM"),  
  rel_coord = c("X_loc", "Y_loc"),
  plot_ID = "Plot",                      
  trust_GPS_corners = FALSE,
  tree_data = data_AGB04,
  tree_coords = c("PX", "PY"),
  tree_plot_ID = "Plot",           
  draw_plot = TRUE, 
  rm_outliers = FALSE
)

fieldplot05 <- check_plot_coord(
  corner_data =  MST_plot_corner,
  proj_coord = c("X_UTM", "Y_UTM"),  
  rel_coord = c("X_loc", "Y_loc"),
  plot_ID = "Plot",                      
  trust_GPS_corners = FALSE,
  tree_data = data_AGB05,
  tree_coords = c("PX", "PY"),
  tree_plot_ID = "Plot",           
  draw_plot = TRUE, 
  rm_outliers = FALSE
)

This similar to the previous check_plot_coord call but now includes tree_data = data_AGB04 (or data_AGB05) and tree_coords = c("PX", "PY") to visualize the spatial distribution of individual trees within the defined plot boundaries for Census 4 and 5. This helps verify that tree locations are correctly mapped within the plot.

st_write(
  obj = fieldplot04$polygon,
  dsn = "C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/MST_fieldplot04.shp",
  delete_layer = TRUE
)
G3;writing: substituting ENGCRS["Undefined Cartesian SRS with unknown unit"] for missing CRS
g
Deleting layer `MST_fieldplot04' using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Writing layer `MST_fieldplot04' to data source 
  `C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/MST_fieldplot04.shp' using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Writing 1 features with 0 fields and geometry type Polygon.
st_write(
  obj = fieldplot05$polygon,
  dsn = "C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/MST_fieldplot05.shp",
  delete_layer = TRUE
)
G3;writing: substituting ENGCRS["Undefined Cartesian SRS with unknown unit"] for missing CRS
g
Deleting layer `MST_fieldplot05' using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Writing layer `MST_fieldplot05' to data source 
  `C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/MST_fieldplot05.shp' using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Writing 1 features with 0 fields and geometry type Polygon.

This block exports the defined polygon of the “MST” field plot as a ESRI Shapefile (.shp), a common geospatial vector data format.

subplots04_grid50 <- divide_plot(
  corner_data = fieldplot$corner_coord, 
  rel_coord = c("x_rel","y_rel"),
  proj_coord = c("x_proj","y_proj"),
  grid_size = 50,
  tree_data = data_AGB04, 
  tree_coords = c("PX","PY")
)

subplots04_grid100 <- divide_plot(
  corner_data = fieldplot$corner_coord, 
  rel_coord = c("x_rel","y_rel"),
  proj_coord = c("x_proj","y_proj"),
  grid_size = 100,
  tree_data = data_AGB04, 
  tree_coords = c("PX","PY")
)
subplots05_grid50 <- divide_plot(
  corner_data = fieldplot$corner_coord, 
  rel_coord = c("x_rel","y_rel"),
  proj_coord = c("x_proj","y_proj"),
  grid_size = 50,
  tree_data = data_AGB05, 
  tree_coords = c("PX","PY")
)

subplots05_grid100 <- divide_plot(
  corner_data = fieldplot$corner_coord, 
  rel_coord = c("x_rel","y_rel"),
  proj_coord = c("x_proj","y_proj"),
  grid_size = 100,
  tree_data = data_AGB05, 
  tree_coords = c("PX","PY")
)

This block divides the main plot (MST) into smaller, regular subplots (grids) and assigns trees to these subplots for the census 4 and 5.

  • divide_plot(...): This function from BIOMASS partitions a larger plot into a grid of subplots. corner_data = fiel

    • dplot$corner_coord: Provides the corrected corner coordinates of the main plot.
    • rel_coord and proj_coord: Specify the relative and projected coordinate columns for the corners.
    • grid_size = 50: Divides the plot into subplots of 50x50 meters (0.25 ha).
    • grid_size = 100: Divides the plot into subplots of 100x100 meters (1 ha).
    • tree_data = data_AGB04 and tree_coords = c("PX","PY"): Assigns each tree from data_AGB04 to its corresponding subplot based on its relative coordinates.

  • subplots04_grid50, subplots04_grid100, subplots05_grid50 and subplots05_grid100, will contain the tree data augmented with subplot_ID and the spatial geometries of these subplots.

subplot_metric04_perHA50 <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots04_grid50,
  value = "AGB",
  per_ha = TRUE)

This block calculates the total AGB per hectare for each 50x50m subplot in the 2004 census.

  • subplot_summary(...): This function aggregates a specified value within each subplot.
-   `subplots = subplots04_grid50`: Provides the divided subplots data.

-   `value = "AGB"`: Specifies that the `AGB` column (calculated previously) should be summarized.

-   `per_ha = TRUE`: Instructs the function to convert the total AGB within each subplot to **Mg per hectare (Mg/ha)**, which is a standardized unit for biomass density.
  • subplot_metric04_perHA50 will contain the summarized AGB values for each subplot, along with their spatial geometries.
subplot_polygons0.25_perHA <- sf::st_set_crs(
 subplot_metric04_perHA50$polygon ,
  value = "EPSG:32647") 

sf::st_write(subplot_polygons0.25_perHA,"C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C4_0.25HA_perHA.shp")
G2;H2;Warningh in abbreviate_shapefile_names(obj) :
  Field names abbreviated for ESRI Shapefile driverg
Layer polygon_C4_0.25HA_perHA in dataset C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C4_0.25HA_perHA.shp already exists:
use either append=TRUE to append to layer or append=FALSE to overwrite layer
G1;H1;Errorh: Dataset already exists.
g
subplot_metric04_sum50<- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots04_grid50,
  value = "AGB",
  fun = quantile, probs = 0.5, # yes, it is the median
  per_ha = FALSE)

This block calculates the median AGB per subplot (not per hectare) for the 50x50m grid in census 4 and exports these subplot polygons as a shapefile.

  • subplot_summary(..., fun = quantile, probs = 0.5, per_ha = FALSE): Calculates the median AGB for each subplot (fun = quantile, probs = 0.5) and per_ha = FALSE means it provides the total AGB within the subplot area, not scaled to per hectare.
subplot_metric04_perHA100 <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots04_grid100,
  value = "AGB", 
  per_ha = TRUE)

subplot_polygons01_perHA <- sf::st_set_crs(
 subplot_metric04_perHA100$polygon ,
  value = "EPSG:32647") 

sf::st_write(subplot_polygons01_perHA,"C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C4_01HA_perHA.shp")
G2;H2;Warningh in abbreviate_shapefile_names(obj) :
  Field names abbreviated for ESRI Shapefile driverg
Layer polygon_C4_01HA_perHA in dataset C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C4_01HA_perHA.shp already exists:
use either append=TRUE to append to layer or append=FALSE to overwrite layer
G1;H1;Errorh: Dataset already exists.
g

This block calculates the total AGB per hectare for each 100x100m subplot in the census 4, similar to subplot_metric04_perHA50 but for the larger grid size.

subplot_metric04_sum100 <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots04_grid100,
  value = "AGB",
  fun = quantile, probs = 0.5,
  per_ha = FALSE)

subplot_polygons01 <- sf::st_set_crs(
  subplot_metric04_sum100$polygon ,
  value = "EPSG:32647")

This block calculates the median AGB per subplot (not per hectare) for the 100x100m grid in census 4 and exports these subplot polygons as a shapefile, similar to the 50x50m version.

subplot_metric05_perHA50  <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots05_grid50,
  value = "AGB", 
  per_ha = TRUE)

subplot_polygons0.25_perHA2 <- sf::st_set_crs(
 subplot_metric05_perHA50$polygon ,
  value = "EPSG:32647") 

sf::st_write(subplot_polygons0.25_perHA,"C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C5_0.25HA_perHA.shp")

  subplots = subplots05_grid50,

subplot_metric05_sum100 <- subplot_summary(

  value = "AGB",

  per_ha = FALSE)

poly04 <- subplot_metric04_sum50$polygon
poly05 <- subplot_metric05_sum50$polygon

Visualizing AGB Change per Subplot (0.25 HA)

poly04_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly04)
poly05_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly05)

agb_change <- poly04_df %>%
  inner_join(poly05_df, by = "subplot_ID", suffix = c("_04", "_05")) %>%
  mutate(AGB_change = AGB_summary_per_ha_05 - AGB_summary_per_ha_04)

agb_map <- left_join(poly04, agb_change, by = "subplot_ID")

agb_map <- poly04 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  inner_join(agb_change, by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  left_join(poly04 %>% dplyr::select(subplot_ID, sf_subplot_polygon), by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  st_as_sf()                           

ggplot(agb_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "red", mid = "white", high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint = 0, name = "AGB Change\n(Mg/ha)"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Change in AGB between Census 04 and Census 05 (1 HA)") +
  theme_minimal()

This complex block calculates the change in AGB per hectare for each 50x50m subplot between census 4 and 5 and then visualizes this change on a spatial map.

  • st_drop_geometry(poly04) and st_drop_geometry(poly05): Removes the spatial geometry from the subplot sf objects, converting them into regular data frames. This is necessary for performing standard data frame joins.

  • agb_change <- ... inner_join(...) %>% mutate(AGB_change = ...): Performs an inner_join on the two dataframes by subplot_ID to match corresponding subplots across years. It then calculates AGB_change by subtracting AGB from census 4 from AGB in census 5.

  • The subsequent lines (starting agb_map <- poly04 %>% ...) re-attach the calculated AGB_change values back to the original sf polygon object of poly04. It ensures that the spatial information is re-associated with the AGB change data.

  • ggplot(agb_map) + geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change), color = "black"): Creates a spatial plot. geom_sf is specifically for plotting simple feature objects. The fill color is mapped to AGB_change.

  • scale_fill_gradient2(...): Sets a diverging color gradient for the fill. low = "red" indicates a decrease, mid = "white" indicates no change, and high = "darkgreen" indicates an increase. midpoint = 0 centers the color scale around zero.

  • labs(...) and theme_minimal(): Sets the plot title, legend title, and theme. This map visually highlights areas of AGB gain or loss within the plot.

ยังไม่ได้เขียนอธิบายกราฟล่าง

agb_change <- poly04_df %>%
  inner_join(poly05_df, by = "subplot_ID", suffix = c("_04", "_05")) %>%
  mutate(

    AGB_change_relative = ifelse(
      AGB_summary_per_ha_04 > 0,
      (AGB_summary_per_ha_05 - AGB_summary_per_ha_04) / AGB_summary_per_ha_04,
      0
    )
  )

agb_map <- poly04 %>%
 st_drop_geometry() %>%
 inner_join(agb_change, by = "subplot_ID") %>%
 left_join(poly04 %>% dplyr::select(subplot_ID, sf_subplot_polygon), by = "subplot_ID") %>%
 st_as_sf()

ggplot(agb_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change_relative), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "red", mid = "white", high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint = 0, name = "Relative AGB Change\n(%)",
    labels = scales::percent 
  ) +
  labs(title = "Relative Change in AGB between Census 04 and Census 05 (0.25 HA)") +
  theme_minimal()

Visualizing AGB Change per Subplot (1 HA)

poly04 <- subplot_metric04_sum100$polygon
poly05 <- subplot_metric05_sum100$polygon
poly04_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly04)
poly05_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly05)

agb_change <- poly04_df %>%
  inner_join(poly05_df, by = "subplot_ID", suffix = c("_04", "_05")) %>%
  mutate(AGB_change = AGB_summary_per_ha_05 - AGB_summary_per_ha_04)

agb_map <- left_join(poly04, agb_change, by = "subplot_ID")

agb_map <- poly04 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%            
  inner_join(agb_change, by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  left_join(poly04 %>% dplyr::select(subplot_ID, sf_subplot_polygon), by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  st_as_sf()                          

ggplot(agb_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "red", mid = "white", high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint = 0, name = "AGB Change\n(Mg/ha)"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Change in AGB between Census 04 and Census 05 (Grid = 100)") +
  theme_minimal()

This block performs the identical calculation and visualization of AGB change per hectare, but for the 100x100m subplots. This allows for a coarser spatial resolution of AGB changes within the plot.

ยังไม่ได้เขียนอธิบายกราฟล่าง

poly04_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly04)
poly05_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly05)

agb_change <- poly04_df %>%
  inner_join(poly05_df, by = "subplot_ID", suffix = c("_04", "_05")) %>%
  mutate(
    AGB_change_relative = ifelse(
      AGB_summary_per_ha_04 > 0,
      (AGB_summary_per_ha_05 - AGB_summary_per_ha_04) / AGB_summary_per_ha_04,
      0
    )
  )

agb_map <- poly04 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  inner_join(agb_change, by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  left_join(poly04 %>% dplyr::select(subplot_ID, sf_subplot_polygon), by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  st_as_sf()

ggplot(agb_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change_relative), color = "black") +
  
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "red", mid = "white", high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint = 0, name = "Relative AGB Change\n(%)",
    labels = scales::percent 
    
  ) +
  
  labs(title = "Relative Change in AGB between Census 04 and Census 05 (1 HA)") +
  theme_minimal()

sf_subplots04_50 <- subplot_metric04_perHA50$polygon
sf_subplots04_100 <- subplot_metric04_perHA100$polygon

sf_subplots05_50 <- subplot_metric05_perHA50$polygon
sf_subplots05_100 <- subplot_metric05_perHA100$polygon
df_100 <- bind_rows(
  sf_subplots04_100 %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(Census = 4),
  sf_subplots05_100 %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(Census = 5)
)

ggplot(df_100, aes(x = AGB_summary_per_ha, fill = factor(Census), color = factor(Census))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(data = df_100 %>% group_by(Census) %>%
               summarise(mu = mean(AGB_summary_per_ha, na.rm = TRUE)),
             aes(xintercept = mu, color = factor(Census)),
             linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("4" = "#2ECC71", "5" = "#E75480")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("4" = "#2ECC71", "5" = "#E75480")) +
  labs(
    title = "Density Plot of AGB per HA by Census (1 HA)",
    x = "AGB per ha",
    fill = "Census",
    color = "Census"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

df_50 <- bind_rows(
  sf_subplots04_50 %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(Census = 4),
  sf_subplots05_50 %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(Census = 5)
)

ggplot(df_50, aes(x = AGB_summary_per_ha, fill = factor(Census), color = factor(Census))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(data = df_50 %>% group_by(Census) %>%
               summarise(mu = mean(AGB_summary_per_ha, na.rm = TRUE)),
             aes(xintercept = mu, color = factor(Census)),
             linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("4" = "#2ECC71", "5" = "#E75480")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("4" = "#2ECC71", "5" = "#E75480")) +
  labs(
    title = "Density Plot of AGB per HA by Census (0.25 HA)",
    x = "AGB per ha",
    fill = "Census",
    color = "Census"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Custom Height Prediction Function

Calculate_Hpred <- function(Plot, DiamManCorr) {
  if (Plot == "MST") {
    return(exp(0.221 + 0.5 * 0.324^2 + 1.115 * log(DiamManCorr) - 0.075 * log(DiamManCorr)^2))
  }
}

This code defines a custom R function named Calculate_Hpred to predict tree height based on diameter (DiamManCorr) for a specific plot (“MST”).

data_AGB04$Hpred <- mapply(Calculate_Hpred, data_AGB04$Plot,data_AGB04$DiamManCorr)
data_AGB05$Hpred <- mapply(Calculate_Hpred, data_AGB05$Plot,data_AGB05$DiamManCorr)
summary(data_AGB04$Hpred)
   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
  6.513   7.938  10.605  13.108  16.112  58.683 
summary(data_AGB05$Hpred)
   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
  6.513   7.938  10.512  13.030  15.897  57.760

This block applies the custom Calculate_Hpred function to predict height for each tree in both census datasets.

  • mapply(Calculate_Hpred, data_AGB04$Plot, data_AGB04$DiamManCorr): The mapply function is used to apply Calculate_Hpred row-wise, passing the Plot and DiamManCorr values for each tree.

  • New columns named Hpred are added to data_AGB04 and data_AGB05 to store these predicted heights.

summary(): Displays a statistical summary of the predicted heights for both years, providing insights into their range and distribution.

Comparing Custom Predicted Height (Hpred) with Chave-estimated Height (H_chave)


ggplot(data_AGB04, aes(x = H_chave, y = Hpred)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "#26C4B8") +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Hpred vs H_chave (Census 4)",
    x = "H_chave (m)",
    y = "Hpred (m)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

This code generates a scatter plot to visually compare the custom predicted heights (Hpred) with the heights estimated using the Chave model (H_chave) for Census 4.

ggplot(data_AGB05, aes(x = H_chave, y = Hpred)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "#E75480") +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Hpred vs H_chave (Census 5)",
    x = "H_chave (m)",
    y = "Hpred (m)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

This code generates the identical scatter plot as above, but for the census 5, allowing comparison of height estimates in that year.

Re-fitting HD Model with Custom Predicted Height

HDmodel04_Hpred <- modelHD(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr,
  H = data_AGB04$Hpred,
  method = "log2",
  useWeight = TRUE
)

HDmodel05_Hpred <- modelHD(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr,
  H = data_AGB05$Hpred,
  method = "log2",
  useWeight = TRUE
)

This block re-fits the Height-Diameter (H-D) model for both census years, but this time using the custom predicted heights (Hpred) instead of the H_chave estimates. This new model will be used for AGB calculations and error propagation based on the custom height function.

Re-computing AGB with Custom Predicted Height

data_AGB04$AGB_by_Hpred <- computeAGB(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr,
  WD = data_AGB04$WD,
  H = data_AGB04$Hpred 
)

data_AGB05$AGB_by_Hpred <- computeAGB(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr,
  WD = data_AGB05$WD,
  H = data_AGB05$Hpred 
)

This block re-calculates the Above Ground Biomass (AGB) for each tree using the custom predicted heights (Hpred) instead of the H_chave estimates.

  • A new column AGB_by_Hpred is added to data_AGB04 and data_AGB05 to store these new AGB values, allowing for comparison with the previous AGB estimates.

Propagating Errors with Custom Predicted Height (Monte Carlo)

These blocks repeat the Monte Carlo error propagation, but now considering the uncertainty associated with the custom predicted heights (Hpred) and their corresponding H-D models.

D_error_prop04_Hpred <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB04$WD, H = data_AGB04$Hpred,
  Dpropag = "chave2004",
  errWD = rep(0,nrow(data_AGB04)), errH = 0 
)

WD_error_prop04_Hpred <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB04$WD, H = data_AGB04$Hpred,
  errWD = wood_densities04$sdWD,
  Dpropag = 0 , errH = 0 
)

H_model_error_prop04_Hpred <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB04$WD, # we do not provide H
  HDmodel = HDmodel04_Hpred, # but we provide HDmodel
  Dpropag = 0 , errWD = rep(0,nrow(data_AGB04)) # no error propagation on D and WD here
)

error_prop04_Hpred <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB04$DiamManCorr,         
  WD =  data_AGB04$WD,        
  H =  data_AGB04$Hpred, 
  Dpropag = "chave2004",           
  errWD = wood_densities04$sdWD,    
  errH = H_model_error_prop04_Hpred$sdAGB
)

This block performs the individual and combined Monte Carlo error propagation for the 2004 census data, using the Hpred values and the HDmodel04_Hpred. It separately assesses errors from DBH, wood density, and the custom height model, then combines them into error_prop04_Hpred.

D_error_prop05_Hpred <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB05$WD, H = data_AGB05$Hpred,
  Dpropag = "chave2004",
  errWD = rep(0,nrow(data_AGB05)), errH = 0 
)

WD_error_prop05_Hpred <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB05$WD, H = data_AGB05$Hpred,
  errWD = wood_densities05$sdWD,
  Dpropag = 0 , errH = 0 
)

H_model_error_prop05_Hpred <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr, WD = data_AGB05$WD, # we do not provide H
  HDmodel = HDmodel05_Hpred, # but we provide HDmodel
  Dpropag = 0 , errWD = rep(0,nrow(data_AGB05)) # no error propagation on D and WD here
)

error_prop05_Hpred <- AGBmonteCarlo(
  D = data_AGB05$DiamManCorr,         
  WD =  data_AGB05$WD,        
  H =  data_AGB05$Hpred, 
  Dpropag = "chave2004",           
  errWD = wood_densities05$sdWD,    
  errH = H_model_error_prop05_Hpred$sdAGB
)

This block performs the identical Monte Carlo error propagation for the census 5 data, using Hpred values and HDmodel05_Hpred.

Displaying Summary of Monte Carlo Results (Hpred)

error_prop04_Hpred[(1:4)]
$meanAGB
[1] 12619.5

$medAGB
[1] 12623.43

$sdAGB
[1] 185.0196

$credibilityAGB
    2.5%    97.5% 
12261.08 12973.81

Displays the first four elements of the error_prop04_Hpred object, showing the summary (mean, standard deviation, confidence intervals) of AGB estimated using the custom predicted heights and their associated propagated errors for census 4.

error_prop05_Hpred[(1:4)]
$meanAGB
[1] 12483.52

$medAGB
[1] 12485.02

$sdAGB
[1] 176.3109

$credibilityAGB
    2.5%    97.5% 
12163.70 12805.13

Displays the first four elements of the error_prop05_Hpred object for the census 5.

Summarizing and Plotting AGB by Plot (Hpred)

AGB_by_plot04_Hpred <- summaryByPlot(AGB_val = error_prop04_Hpred$AGB_simu, plot = data_AGB04$Plot, drawPlot = TRUE)

AGB_by_plot05_Hpred <- summaryByPlot(AGB_val = error_prop05_Hpred$AGB_simu, plot = data_AGB05$Plot, drawPlot = TRUE)

This block summarizes the AGB estimates at the plot level using the Monte Carlo simulation results derived from Hpred.

  • summaryByPlot(...): Aggregates the simulated AGB values (AGB_simu) from error_prop04_Hpred and error_prop05_Hpred by plot, providing mean AGB and confidence intervals.

AGB_by_plot04_Hpred$Census <- "4"
AGB_by_plot05_Hpred$Census <- "5"

AGB_combined_Hpred <- bind_rows(AGB_by_plot04_Hpred, AGB_by_plot05_Hpred)

ggplot(AGB_combined_Hpred, aes(x = Census, y = AGB, color = Census)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Cred_2.5, ymax = Cred_97.5), width = 0.2) +
  labs(
    title = "AGB Comparison for Plot MST",
    x = "Census",
    y = "AGB (Mg/ha)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("5" = "#E75480", "4" = "#2ECC71"))

This block prepares and visualizes the plot-level AGB with confidence intervals for both census years, specifically for the AGB estimated using Hpred.

  • It adds a Census column to the plot summaries and combines them.

  • A ggplot generates a point plot with error bars, similar to the earlier AGB comparison plot, but now reflecting the AGB calculated using the custom height prediction. This allows for a direct comparison of total AGB and its uncertainty between the two census years based on this alternative height estimation method.

Subplot Division with Custom Predicted Height (Hpred)

subplots04_grid50_Hpred <- divide_plot(
  corner_data = fieldplot$corner_coord, 
  rel_coord = c("x_rel","y_rel"),
  proj_coord = c("x_proj","y_proj"),
  grid_size = 50,
  tree_data = data_AGB04, 
  tree_coords = c("PX","PY")
)

subplots04_grid100_Hpred <- divide_plot(
  corner_data = fieldplot$corner_coord, 
  rel_coord = c("x_rel","y_rel"),
  proj_coord = c("x_proj","y_proj"),
  grid_size = 100,
  tree_data = data_AGB04, 
  tree_coords = c("PX","PY")
)
subplots05_grid50_Hpred <- divide_plot(
  corner_data = fieldplot$corner_coord, 
  rel_coord = c("x_rel","y_rel"),
  proj_coord = c("x_proj","y_proj"),
  grid_size = 50,
  tree_data = data_AGB05, 
  tree_coords = c("PX","PY")
)

subplots05_grid100_Hpred <- divide_plot(
  corner_data = fieldplot$corner_coord, 
  rel_coord = c("x_rel","y_rel"),
  proj_coord = c("x_proj","y_proj"),
  grid_size = 100,
  tree_data = data_AGB05, 
  tree_coords = c("PX","PY")
)

This block re-divides the main plot into 50x50m and 100x100m subplots for both census years. Although the function call here uses data_AGB04 and data_AGB05, which now contain AGB_by_Hpred, the divide_plot function itself primarily assigns trees to subplots based on their coordinates, not their AGB values directly. The new AGB values based on Hpred will be used in subsequent subplot_summary calls.

Summarizing AGB per Subplot with Custom Predicted Height (Hpred)

subplot_metric04_perHA50_Hpred <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots04_grid50_Hpred,
  value = "AGB_by_Hpred",
  per_ha = TRUE)


subplot_polygons0.25_perHA_Hpred <- sf::st_set_crs(
 subplot_metric04_perHA50_Hpred$polygon ,
  value = "EPSG:32647") 

sf::st_write(subplot_polygons0.25_perHA_Hpred,"C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C4_0.25HA_perHA_Hpred.shp")
G2;H2;Warningh in abbreviate_shapefile_names(obj) :
  Field names abbreviated for ESRI Shapefile driverg
Layer polygon_C4_0.25HA_perHA_Hpred in dataset C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C4_0.25HA_perHA_Hpred.shp already exists:
use either append=TRUE to append to layer or append=FALSE to overwrite layer
G1;H1;Errorh: Dataset already exists.
g

This block calculates the total AGB per hectare for each 50x50m subplot in the 2004 census, specifically using the AGB values calculated with the custom predicted heights (AGB_by_Hpred).

subplot_metric04_sum50_Hpred <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots04_grid50_Hpred,
  value = "AGB_by_Hpred",
  fun = quantile, probs = 0.5,
  per_ha = FALSE)

This block calculates the median AGB per subplot for the 50x50m grid in 2004.

subplot_metric04_perHA100_Hpred <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots04_grid100_Hpred,
  value = "AGB_by_Hpred",
  per_ha = TRUE)

subplot_polygons01_perHA_Hpred <- sf::st_set_crs(
 subplot_metric04_perHA100_Hpred$polygon ,
  value = "EPSG:32647") 

sf::st_write(subplot_polygons01_perHA_Hpred,"C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C4_01HA_perHA_Hpred.shp")
G2;H2;Warningh in abbreviate_shapefile_names(obj) :
  Field names abbreviated for ESRI Shapefile driverg
Layer polygon_C4_01HA_perHA_Hpred in dataset C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C4_01HA_perHA_Hpred.shp already exists:
use either append=TRUE to append to layer or append=FALSE to overwrite layer
G1;H1;Errorh: Dataset already exists.
g

This block calculates the total AGB per hectare for each 100x100m subplot in the 2004 census, using AGB_by_Hpred.

subplot_metric04_sum100_Hpred <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots04_grid100_Hpred,
  value = "AGB_by_Hpred",
  fun = quantile, probs = 0.5,
  per_ha = FALSE)

This block calculates the median AGB per subplot for the 100x100m grid in 2004. Note: Similar to the 50m grid, this summary uses the “AGB” column instead of “AGB_by_Hpred”.

subplot_metric05_perHA50_Hpred <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots05_grid50_Hpred,
  value = "AGB_by_Hpred",
  per_ha = TRUE)

subplot_polygons0.25_perHA_Hpred2 <- sf::st_set_crs(
 subplot_metric05_perHA50_Hpred$polygon ,
  value = "EPSG:32647") 

sf::st_write(subplot_polygons0.25_perHA_Hpred2,"C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C5_0.25HA_perHA_Hpred.shp")
G2;H2;Warningh in abbreviate_shapefile_names(obj) :
  Field names abbreviated for ESRI Shapefile driverg
Layer polygon_C5_0.25HA_perHA_Hpred in dataset C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C5_0.25HA_perHA_Hpred.shp already exists:
use either append=TRUE to append to layer or append=FALSE to overwrite layer
G1;H1;Errorh: Dataset already exists.
g

This block calculates the total AGB per hectare for each 50x50m subplot in the Census 5, using AGB_by_Hpred.

subplot_metric05_sum50_Hpred <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots05_grid50_Hpred,
  value = "AGB_by_Hpred",
  fun = quantile, probs = 0.5,
  per_ha = FALSE)

This block calculates the median AGB per subplot for the 50x50m grid in Census 5.

subplot_metric05_perHA100_Hpred <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots05_grid100_Hpred,
  value = "AGB_by_Hpred", # AGB was added before applying divide_plot()
  per_ha = TRUE)

subplot_polygons01_perHA_Hpred2 <- sf::st_set_crs(
 subplot_metric05_perHA100_Hpred$polygon ,
  value = "EPSG:32647") 

sf::st_write(subplot_polygons01_perHA_Hpred2,"C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C5_01HA_perHA_Hpred.shp")
G2;H2;Warningh in abbreviate_shapefile_names(obj) :
  Field names abbreviated for ESRI Shapefile driverg
Layer polygon_C5_01HA_perHA_Hpred in dataset C:/Users/rakir/OneDrive/Desktop/AGB_Project/Field/shp/polygon_C5_01HA_perHA_Hpred.shp already exists:
use either append=TRUE to append to layer or append=FALSE to overwrite layer
G1;H1;Errorh: Dataset already exists.
g

This block calculates the total AGB per hectare for each 100x100m subplot in the 2005 census, using AGB_by_Hpred.

subplot_metric05_sum100_Hpred <- subplot_summary(
  subplots = subplots05_grid100_Hpred,
  value = "AGB_by_Hpred",
  fun = quantile, probs = 0.5, # yes, it is the median
  per_ha = FALSE)

This block calculates the median AGB per subplot for the 100x100m grid in census 5.

Visualizing AGB Change per Subplot (0.25 HA, Hpred)

poly04 <- subplot_metric04_perHA50_Hpred$polygon
poly05 <- subplot_metric05_perHA50_Hpred$polygon

This block retrieves the sf polygon objects for the 50x50m subplots from the AGB per hectare summaries calculated using AGB_by_Hpred.

poly04_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly04)
poly05_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly05)

agb_change <- poly04_df %>%
  inner_join(poly05_df, by = "subplot_ID", suffix = c("_04", "_05")) %>%
  mutate(AGB_change = AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_05 - AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_04)

agb_map <- left_join(poly04, agb_change, by = "subplot_ID")

agb_map <- poly04 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  inner_join(agb_change, by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  left_join(poly04 %>%  dplyr::select(subplot_ID, sf_subplot_polygon), by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  st_as_sf()        

ggplot(agb_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "red", mid = "white", high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint = 0, name = "AGB Change\n(Mg/ha)"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Change in AGB between Census 04 and Census 05 (0.25 HA)") +
  theme_minimal()

This block calculates and visualizes the change in AGB per hectare for each 50x50m subplot between census 4 and 5, using the AGB values derived from the custom predicted heights (AGB_by_Hpred). The process is identical to the previous AGB change map but uses the AGB values incorporating Hpred.

# --- ส่วนของการเตรียมข้อมูลยังคงเดิม ---
poly04_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly04)
poly05_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly05)

# --- ปรับแก้ส่วนของการคำนวณ ---
agb_change <- poly04_df %>%
  inner_join(poly05_df, by = "subplot_ID", suffix = c("_04", "_05")) %>%
  mutate(
    # คำนวณการเปลี่ยนแปลงเชิงสัมพัทธ์ (Relative Change) เป็นทศนิยม
    # เพิ่ม ifelse เพื่อป้องกันการหารด้วยศูนย์
    AGB_change_relative = ifelse(
      AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_04 > 0,
      (AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_05 - AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_04) / AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_04,
      0
    )
  )

# --- ส่วนของการ join กลับไปยังข้อมูล sf ยังคงเดิม ---
agb_map <- poly04 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  inner_join(agb_change, by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  left_join(poly04 %>%  dplyr::select(subplot_ID, sf_subplot_polygon), by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  st_as_sf()      

# --- ปรับแก้ส่วนของการพล็อตกราฟ ---
ggplot(agb_map) +
  # เปลี่ยนมาใช้คอลัมน์ AGB_change_relative
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change_relative), color = "black") +
  
  # ปรับแก้ scale เพื่อจัดรูปแบบแกนสี
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "red", mid = "white", high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint = 0, 
    name = "Relative AGB Change", # เปลี่ยนชื่อแกน
    labels = scales::percent      # เพิ่มบรรทัดนี้เพื่อจัดรูปแบบเป็น %
  ) +
  
  # ปรับแก้ชื่อกราฟ
  labs(title = "Relative Change in AGB between Census 04 and Census 05 (0.25 HA)") +
  theme_minimal()


# หากยังไม่ได้ติดตั้ง package 'scales' ให้รันคำสั่งนี้ใน Console หนึ่งครั้ง:
# install.packages("scales")

Visualizing AGB Change per Subplot (1 HA, Hpred)

poly04 <- subplot_metric04_perHA100_Hpred$polygon
poly05 <- subplot_metric05_perHA100_Hpred$polygon

This block retrieves the sf polygon objects for the 100x100m subplots from the AGB per hectare summaries calculated using AGB_by_Hpred.

poly04_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly04)
poly05_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly05)

agb_change <- poly04_df %>%
  inner_join(poly05_df, by = "subplot_ID", suffix = c("_04", "_05")) %>%
  mutate(AGB_change = AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_05 - AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_04)


agb_map <- left_join(poly04, agb_change, by = "subplot_ID")

agb_map <- poly04 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%          
  inner_join(agb_change, by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  left_join(poly04 %>%  dplyr::select(subplot_ID, sf_subplot_polygon), by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  st_as_sf()          

ggplot(agb_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change), color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "red", mid = "white", high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint = 0, name = "AGB Change\n(Mg/ha)"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Change in AGB between Census 04 and Census 05 (1 HA)") +
  theme_minimal()

This final block calculates and visualizes the change in AGB per hectare for each 100x100m subplot between census 4 and census 5, using the AGB values derived from the custom predicted heights (AGB_by_Hpred). This provides a coarser-resolution spatial map of AGB change, serving as a final visualization of the forest’s biomass dynamics.

# --- Data preparation remains the same ---
poly04_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly04)
poly05_df <- st_drop_geometry(poly05)

# --- 1. Adjust the calculation to be relative ---
agb_change <- poly04_df %>%
  inner_join(poly05_df, by = "subplot_ID", suffix = c("_04", "_05")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate relative change: (new - old) / old
    # ifelse prevents division by zero
    AGB_change_relative = ifelse(
      AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_04 > 0,
      (AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_05 - AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_04) / AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha_04,
      0
    )
  )

# --- Rejoining spatial data remains the same ---
agb_map <- poly04 %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  inner_join(agb_change, by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  left_join(poly04 %>%  dplyr::select(subplot_ID, sf_subplot_polygon), by = "subplot_ID") %>%
  st_as_sf()

# --- 2. Adjust the plot to show relative change ---
ggplot(agb_map) +
  # Use the new 'AGB_change_relative' column for the fill
  geom_sf(aes(fill = AGB_change_relative), color = "black") +
  
  # Update the scale and add percentage labels
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "red", mid = "white", high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint = 0, 
    name = "Relative AGB Change",
    labels = scales::percent # This formats labels as percentages
  ) +
  
  # Update the title
  labs(title = "Relative Change in AGB between Census 04 and Census 05 (1 HA)") +
  theme_minimal()


# Note: You may need to run install.packages("scales") if you haven't already.
sf_subplots04_50_Hpred <- subplot_metric04_perHA50_Hpred$polygon
sf_subplots04_100_Hpred <- subplot_metric04_perHA100_Hpred$polygon

sf_subplots05_50_Hpred <- subplot_metric05_perHA50_Hpred$polygon
sf_subplots05_100_Hpred <- subplot_metric05_perHA100_Hpred$polygon
df_100_Hpred <- bind_rows(
  sf_subplots04_100_Hpred %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(Census = 4),
  sf_subplots05_100_Hpred %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(Census = 5)
)

df_50_Hpred <- bind_rows(
  sf_subplots04_50_Hpred %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(Census = 4),
  sf_subplots05_50_Hpred %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(Census = 5)
)

ggplot(df_100_Hpred, aes(x = AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha, fill = factor(Census), color = factor(Census))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(data = df_100_Hpred %>% group_by(Census) %>%
               summarise(mu = mean(AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha, na.rm = TRUE)),
             aes(xintercept = mu, color = factor(Census)),
             linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("4" = "#2ECC71", "5" = "#E75480")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("4" = "#2ECC71", "5" = "#E75480")) +
  labs(
    title = "Density Plot of AGB_Hpred per ha by Census (1 HA)",
    x = "AGB per ha",
    fill = "Census",
    color = "Census"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


ggplot(df_50_Hpred, aes(x = AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha, fill = factor(Census), color = factor(Census))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(data = df_50_Hpred %>% group_by(Census) %>%
               summarise(mu = mean(AGB_by_Hpred_summary_per_ha, na.rm = TRUE)),
             aes(xintercept = mu, color = factor(Census)),
             linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("4" = "#2ECC71", "5" = "#E75480")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("4" = "#2ECC71", "5" = "#E75480")) +
  labs(
    title = "Density Plot of AGB_Hpred per ha by Census (0.25 HA)",
    x = "AGB per ha",
    fill = "Census",
    color = "Census"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
